comments.text: Worth noting: the best current lower bound is due to Raigorodskii (2000), improving Frankl–Wilson to \(\chi(G_n)\ge (1.239\ldots+o(1))^n\). (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

I asked GPT 5.4 to milk the proof in Raigorodskii (2000). No success, but at least it gave a more precise value, namely Gamma (x*,y*) = 1.2395667407265985… Gemini 3.1 Pro had come up with the weaker constant 1.239561.. I asked both models to recheck. GPT 5.4 stayed at its value, and Gemini 3.1 Pro accepted at least 1.23956674.
Do you mean these are exactly the same as Raigorodskii's result, but computed with higher precisions? If so, *which* value is correct? How can we be certain?

Here is a link to my chat with Gemini 3.1 Pro. First it had rounding errors, and in a second run (with higher arithmetic precision) it claimed that 1.23956674.. was correct: https://gemini.google.com/share/8d073b94839c
